channel.title: Service-Error on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Service-Error on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] Kubernetes No Endpoints Available — service has no endpoints
item.description:
Kubernetes No Endpoints Available — service has no endpoints

A service shows no endpoints when its selector doesn’t match any running pods. Traffic to the service will fail.

Common Causes

  • Service selector doesn’t match pod labels
  • No pods are running for the service
  • Pods are in a non-Running state
  • Selector label is misspelled

How to Fix

Check Service Selector

kubectl get svc <service-name> -o yaml

Check Matching Pods

kubectl get pods -l <selector-key>=<selector-value>

Verify Endpoints

kubectl get endpoints <service-name>

Fix Pod Labels

metadata:
 labels:
 app: my-service  # Must match service selector

Describe Service for Events

kubectl describe svc <service-name>

Check Pod Status

kubectl get pods --show-labels

Examples

# Example 1: Label mismatch
kubectl get endpoints my-svc
# NAME ENDPOINTS AGE
# my-svc <none> 5m
# Fix: ensure pods have label matching service selector

# Example 2: Pods not running
kubectl get pods -l app=my-service
# NAME READY STATUS
# my-service-pod 0/1 CrashLoopBackOff
# Fix: resolve pod crash issue

# Example 3: Check all labels
kubectl get pods --show-labels
# Fix: update deployment labels to match service selector

Related Errors

  • Service Error — service endpoint issues
  • Pod Crash — pod keeps crashing

item.title: [Solution] Windows Update Error 0x800f0922 CBS_E_CONNECTOR_DISABLED Fix
item.description:
[Solution] Windows Update Error 0x800f0922 CBS_E_CONNECTOR_DISABLED Fix

Error 0x800f0922 is a Windows Update error with the internal code CBS_E_CONNECTOR_DISABLED. It occurs when the Windows Update servicing stack is disabled or the system cannot connect to the Component-Based Servicing (CBS) component. The update agent fails to download or install updates because its connector has been deactivated.

This error affects both Windows 10 and 11 and is commonly caused by disabled Windows Update services, corrupted update components, or network restrictions blocking Microsoft’s update endpoints.

item.title: [Solution] Error 0x800705b4 Windows 11/10 — Operation Timed Out Fix
item.description:
[Solution] Error 0x800705b4 Windows 11/10 — Operation Timed Out Fix

Error 0x800705b4 is an Operation Timed Out error that occurs when a Windows process fails to complete within its allotted time limit. The system abandons the operation and reports the timeout rather than continuing indefinitely.

This error affects both Windows 10 and 11 and commonly appears during Windows Update, software installation, device driver operations, and network communications.

Description

The full error message typically reads:
